def: s.m. Chain; series; concatenation; line; succession; descent, pedigree, genealogy; a catalogue; arrangement; connection; mode, way
more: n. m. سل running down in a stream. 1. A chain; series; line; succession. 2. Pedigree; genealogy. 3. Arrangement ( tartīb ). 4. Way; mode ( ḍhaṅg , 1). Koī silsilah inke vāste bhī nikāl do! 5. ( Math .) A series. silsilah-bandī , n. f. Arrangement; classification. silsilah-i-jamā o tafrīq ; S. योगांतर श्रेठी; H. ghaṭtī baṛhtī lāg . Arithmetical progression. silsilah-i-khāndān, yā qarābat , Genealogy; genealogical tree. silsilah-i-rayāzī , n. m. Mathematical progression. silsilah-i-zarab o taqsīm ; S. गुणोतर भागोतर श्रेटी; H. gun bhāg lāg , Geometrical progression. silsilah-i-mūsīqī ; S. राग गायण श्रेणी Harmonical progression. silsilah-vār , adv. Linked together as a chain; in regular order; in a series; seriatim ; systematically ( tartībwār ). silsilah-vār, silsilah-band , adj. 1. Consecutive; serial; lineal. 2. Unbroken; uninterrupted. pahāṛoṅ kā silsilah, silsilah-i-koh , A chain of mou…
s. m. A chain, series, concatenation; succession, descent of a family, pedigree, genealogy. سلسله مو silsila-mū, Having the hair in ringlets like chains: (Pers. plur. سلسله مويان).

ety: usually named sīn-ě-mǒhmala, or sīn-ě-g̠air-manqūt̤a, 'the unpointed s'
def: s.m. The letter or the sound sa.
more: usually named سين مـﮩمله the 12th letter in the Arabic, and 15th in the Persian alphabet, corresponds to स in Sauskrit. Its sound is that of the English s in sun. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 60. In almanacs it represents the sun, and a sextile aspect of the planets. It is also an abbreviation for the salutation salām . It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with sh , as syām=shām black; sudh=shudh correct; parsād=parshād offering; dos=dosh sin: with h , as sai=hai is; S. sapt =P. haft =Lat. hepta seven; sastā = E. sahtā cheap; sāsrā =Rus. sāhrā mother-in-law's house; H. das =P. dah ten; with k , as H. sikilnā=khisalnā slide; P. saikal =P. kaigal plaster; saraknā=khisaknā move; bhūṅsnā= bhauṅknā bark; dīsnā=dīkhnā seen; ṭhes= ṭhek stumble: with chh , as P. sāyā = H. chhāyā shade; sai=chhai is; kasnā=kachhnā tighten; pastānā=pachhtānā regret; lālsā=lālach covetousness: w…

the twelfth letter in the Arabic and fifteenth in the Persian alphabet, corresponds to स in Sanskrit. Its sound is that of the English s in sun, sin. In denoting numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 60. In almanacks it represents the Sun, and a sextile aspect of the planets. It is also used as an abbreviation for the salutation السلام. It is changed (in Arabic) (1) into ي as سادي for سادس The sixth: (2) into ت as طست for طسّ A bason: (3) into ص as صلخ for سلخ The last day of the month; and (in Pers.) اصفـہ‍ان for اسپـہ‍ان: (4) into ا as دساها for دسسها He exposed her to deceit: (5) (in Arab. and Pers.) into ز; see ز: (6) (in Pers.) into چ as خروچ for خروس A cock: (7) into ش as فرشته for فرسته Sent: (8) into ه as آماه for آماس A swelling: this last substitution, also, takes place in the aorist of Persian verbs which have س before تن of the infinitive, as خواهم from…
(in Braj) postpos. From, by, with, of; as जासु jā-su, From or of whom. It is, also, sometimes used for سو so, He, she, it, they, &c.

(It is said of a miserly Banyā that he allowed his family only as much ghī (butter) as could be drawn up on a broomstick. He was however outdone by his son who used to place a sealed phial of butter before his family to be looked at only. (Comp. The Irish 'praties and pint,' and the story of the Bengali miser who sat by the river side with his platter of rice and, at each mouthful, pointing to the river, exclaimed ' Ū moshlī, ī bhāt .' There's the fish, and here's the rice).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِپَاهِی (sipāhī). First attested in c. 1635 Middle Hindi سپاہی (sipāhī). By surface analysis, سِپاہ (sipāh, “army”) + ی (ī).

def: n. m. 1. A native soldier ( kālā ), in contradistinction to a European soldier ( gorā ). 2. A beadle; a peon or messenger of a court. 1. Naë sipāhī, mūchh meṅ ḍhāṭā . Prov. A soldier new, moustache askew. 2. Sipāhī kī roṭī sir beche kī . Prov. The soldier gets his bread by risking of his head. 3. Sipahiyā! mohe tāke tu kaisī najrī? Chār rupaiyā kī torī chakaryā, Jhulnī gaṛhaibe, ki uṭhaibe bakhrī? Song. E. Ṭhumrī . Why, soldier! cast those amorous looks at me? Your monthly wage but four rupees you see, Whence jewels and a house for me prythee? sipāhiyān'ah , adj. Like a soldier; soldierly.
adj. Like a soldier, soldierly.

def: s.f. A road, way; — name of the bridge over the midst of Hell across which good Moslems expect to pass into Paradise (it is said to be sharper than a sword and thinner than a hair)

subs: s.f. The right way, i.e. the Moḥammadan religion.

ety: for A. سيرة, v.n. fr. سير 'to go,' &c.; see sair
def: s.f. Way of life, conduct, character, nature, disposition; virtue, morals; qualities (syn. ḵẖaṣlat).
more: n. f. Quality (خصلت). Sīrat ke ham gulām haiṅ, sūrat huī to kyā! Virtue's slave am I, beauty is naught.
(from سير) s. f. Way of life, morals, manners, conduct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِیرَت (sīrat), from Arabic سِيرَة (sīra).

def: adj. Cold, becoming cold.

ety: for A. سراية, inf. n. of سري 'to travel by night,' &c.
def: s.f. Travelling by night; nightly journey; passing from one thing (or part) to another, penetrating, penetration, pervading, creeping (in or through); contagion, infection
more: n. f. سير went. 1. Passing from one thing to another; penetration. 2. Contagion; infection; pestilence. sarāyat k. , v. n. To run through or corrupt the blood.
s. f. Passing from one thing to another, penetrating, a nightly journey, contagion, infection.
subs: (-meṅ), To creep (in or through), to pervade, permeate; to circulate; to extend (to); — to affect by contagion, to infect, to corrupt, taint.

def: s. (dakh. i. q. بيزاري) Displeasure, disgust.
adv. Firstly; imprimis .
adj. & adv. Pure, unmixed, unadulterated; neat; sheer; mere; — purely, merely, only, solely, alone, exclusively, &c.
more: adj. صرف pure. Mere; unmixed (خالي 6.). sirf , adv. Only; merely; purely; solely; but; exclusively. sirf dastkhat . A blank endorsement; carte blanche. sirf sahīh yā daskhat k. , To endorse in blank.

adv. Purely, merely, only, solely, alone. adj. Pure, mere. ¶ ṣarf, s. m. 1. Expenditure. 2. The orthographical and etymological parts of grammar, accidence. 3. Dexterity. 4. Increase, gain, excess. 5. Changing, turning, converting, volubility. 6. Vicissitudes of fortune. صرف کرنا ṣarf karnā, To spend, disburse, to pass, employ. صرف ونحو ṣarf-o-naḥo, s. f. Grammar. صرف هونا ṣarf honā, To be expended.
subs: s.m. 'A signature alone'; a blank endorsement; carte blanche.
ex: مجھے صرف دو اور روپے کی ضرورت ہے۔
mujhe sirf do aur rupe kī zarūrat hai.
I only need two more rupees.
